topicDefinition.oneLiner: Professional video editing made easy with AI. Auto-subtitles, background removal, and smart editing features.
boundaries: Must have timeline or transcript-based editing capabilities.
Must use AI to automate tedious editing tasks (e.g., filler word removal, auto-captions, scene detection).
Excludes purely generative tools that only output raw footage without built-in editing.

howToChoose.title: Evaluate the Editing Interface
howToChoose.desc: Do you prefer text-based editing (like Descript), traditional timeline editing (like Premiere Pro), or mobile-first template editing (like CapCut)?

howToChoose.title: Check Long-Form vs. Short-Form Capabilities
howToChoose.desc: Tools like Opus Clip specialize in repurposing long videos into shorts, while others like DaVinci Resolve are meant for full-length timeline mastery.

howToChoose.title: Assess Credit Limits vs. Media Hours
howToChoose.desc: Understand how the tool prices your usage—some charge by the hour of footage uploaded, while others charge by AI processing credits.

sources.title: The 11 Best AI Video Editors in 2025, Tried and Tested
sources.excerpt: A deep dive into the best AI video editors, categorizing them by professional use, quick edits, and content repurposing.

sections.heading: Top Picks by Use Case
sections.content: For Professionals: Adobe Premiere Pro leads with AI features like scene edit detection. For Text-Based Editing: Descript excels at editing video through transcripts. For Content Repurposing: Opus Clip turns long videos into short, shareable clips.

keyClaims: Leads with AI features like scene edit detection, speech-to-text, auto reframe, and object removal
proofSnippets: Adobe Premiere Pro leads with AI features like scene edit detection, speech-to-text, auto reframe, and object removal, though it has a steep learning curve and costs $22.99+/month.

notes: A highly reputable social media tool blog offering practical marketing tool recommendations.

sources.title: Opus Clip vs CapCut vs Descript: Best AI for Viral Videos
sources.excerpt: Comparing the top three AI video editors for short-form creators and podcasters.

sections.heading: Best For Each Tool
sections.content: OpusClip is best for turning long videos into multiple short clips at scale, with 90%+ AI accuracy. CapCut is the most flexible all-in-one editor, best for short-form creators who want templates, effects, and fast turnaround with strong mobile editing capabilities.

sections.heading: Pricing
sections.content: Descript pricing: Free (60 minutes/month), Hobbyist ($16/month), Creator ($24/month). OpusClip: Free tier with 300 credits; paid plans start around $15/month.

toolMentions.bestFor: Short-form creators seeking templates and effects
keyClaims: Strong mobile and desktop editing capabilities
pricingClaims: Offers strong free value with mobile and desktop options
Paid plans start at $7.99/month (Pro tier is $15/month annually)
proofSnippets: CapCut is the most flexible all-in-one editor, best for short-form creators who want templates, effects, and fast turnaround with strong mobile editing capabilities.

notes: Provides excellent specific pricing and feature breakdown for the top modern AI editors.

sources.title: Opus Clip Official Documentation
sources.excerpt: Official pricing, limits, and refund policy for Opus Clip.

sections.heading: Pricing Plans
sections.content: Free: $0/month with 60 processing minutes. Starter: $15/month with 150 processing minutes. Pro: $29/month with 300 processing minutes.

keyClaims: Automatically detects highlights and formats them for multiple platforms
refundRules: Pro users can refund individual projects within 14 days of upload if no clips have been downloaded yet to restore credits.
limitations: Free tier limited to 60 processing minutes per month
proofSnippets: Pro users can refund individual projects within 14 days of upload if no clips have been downloaded yet.

notes: Official documentation provides highest confidence for limits and refunds.

sources.title: Descript Official Pricing and Limits
sources.excerpt: Official details on Descript plans, credits, and media limits.

sections.heading: Pricing Plans
sections.content: Hobbyist: $16/month (10 media hours, 400 AI credits). Creator: $24/month (30 media hours, 800 AI credits). Business: $50/month (40 media hours, 1500 AI credits).

limitations: Monthly allocations of media minutes and AI credits do not roll over month-to-month
proofSnippets: Plans include monthly allocations of media minutes and AI credits that don't roll over month-to-month.

notes: Official pricing page ensures accurate constraints.
